This study aims to address these challenges by creating a unified database, the CaCS database, that consolidates all open-source CaCS data. This database serves as a central hub for data storage and analysis, designed for seamless integration and utilization within ongoing research projects. It offers users a programmatic interface to retrieve data for tasks such as predicting chemical synthesis outcomes, retrosynthetic analysis, estimating compound synthesizability, and optimizing chemical synthesis routes. The database preserves the original data for reusability and traceability in subsequent tasks and stores processed data more efficiently. The benefits and drawbacks of the database are demonstrated through a practical case study within a contemporary CaCS research project. The CaCS database's code and documentation are accessible on GitHub under the MIT license at https://github.com/neo-chem-synth-wave/ncsw-data. The primary scientific contribution of this research is the consolidation of all relevant open-source CaCS data into a comprehensive database, offering a systematic overview of existing open data sources and a transparent, reproducible environment for data processing and benchmarking purposes. © The Author(s) 2025.

In the global health landscape, breast cancer (BC) remains a leading cause of mortality among women, underscoring the importance of early and accurate diagnosis for effective treatment and survival. This study proposes an advanced BC classification model that leverages a hybrid Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO) optimization technique following the stacking of multiple Machine Learning (ML) models. The research utilizes a publicly available BC dataset from Kaggle, comprising 4024 patient records and 16 attributes, after pre-processing. For feature selection, binary versions of PSO, GWO, and Whale Optimization Algorithm (BWAO) were employed, with PSO demonstrating the optimal fit (0.92025). The proposed approach was evaluated using six ML models, including Random Forest (RF) Classifier, Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD) Classifier, Support Vector Machine (SVM), Gradient Boosting Classifier (GBC), k-Nearest Neighbors (k-NN), XGBoost Classifier (XGB), and Naïve Bayes Classifier (NBC). The RF model achieved the highest accuracy of 89.22%. The stacked models (RF, SGB, SVM, GB, and K-NN) served as base learners, with XGB as the meta-learner. The results indicated a substantial improvement in classification accuracy of 93.91% with the stacked model. Further optimization of the stacked model was achieved using the hybrid PSO-GWO algorithm, resulting in a significant increase in accuracy to 99.49%. The evaluation metrics used in this study include accuracy, precision, recall, F score, and AUC. This research demonstrates the potential of the hybrid PSO-GWO algorithm in optimizing the stacked ML model for improved BC classification accuracy.

In the medical field, liver tumors exhibit substantial diversity in origin, histology, and response to treatment, necessitating precise differential diagnosis for effective management. While multi-phase contrast-enhanced computed tomography (CT) offers valuable imaging patterns for differentiation, visual assessment alone can be limited due to overlapping features. To overcome this limitation, this study introduces MCT-LTDiag, a comprehensive dataset for Liver Tumor Diagnosis, encompassing 517 cases with four-phase contrast-enhanced CT scans (non-contrast, arterial, portal venous, and delayed phases). The dataset includes five liver tumor subtypes: hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC), intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma (ICC), colorectal liver metastasis (CRLM), breast cancer liver metastasis (BCLM), and hepatic hemangioma (HH). MCT-LTDiag undergoes standardized preprocessing, stringent quality control, and expert-annotated tumor masks. Preliminary experiments employing radiomics-based machine learning and deep learning models demonstrate the dataset's utility, with multi-phase integration leading to enhanced diagnostic performance. MCT-LTDiag serves as a valuable benchmark for advancing automated liver tumor subtype classification and is publicly accessible to foster future research in the field. © The Author(s) 2025.

In the contemporary healthcare landscape, the escalating prevalence of multimorbidity poses significant challenges due to increased resource consumption and disjointed care. To alleviate these issues, it is crucial to pinpoint multimorbidity patients with high healthcare utilization for improved management and resource allocation. This study endeavors to classify and profile such patients based on their chronic conditions and healthcare utilization patterns. Employing a weighted K-means approach, we analyzed a population of 1,184,334 individuals, each diagnosed with two or more of 33 chronic conditions, as determined by diagnostic algorithms based on ICD-10 and ATC codes. Sociodemographic factors were utilized to delineate the distinct clusters. Our analysis revealed four distinct clusters. Cluster 1, characterized by a high burden of both physical and mental health issues, combined with a low socioeconomic status, demonstrated the highest healthcare utilization. Cluster 2, predominantly comprising younger women with mental health issues, exhibited a high demand for psychological services with fewer physical health conditions. The largest cluster, cluster 3, consisted of individuals with common, manageable conditions, a relatively high socioeconomic status, and low healthcare utilization. Cluster 4 was defined by older individuals with complex physical health conditions, requiring frequent interaction with general practitioners and specialists. In conclusion, the identified clusters exhibit diverse patterns of chronic conditions and healthcare utilization. The findings emphasize the necessity of personalized strategies, particularly for multimorbidity patients with mental health issues, taking socioeconomic status into account, to enhance care delivery and optimize resource utilization more effectively.

In the burgeoning field of clinical research, artificial intelligence (AI) is being leveraged to enhance diagnostic precision, model prognoses, and monitor diseases, particularly in data-scarce, complex domains such as rare diseases. One such disease, Myasthenia Gravis (MG), a neuromuscular disorder, has witnessed an escalating number of AI-driven studies aiming to address its diagnostic and clinical heterogeneity. However, the methodological quality and reporting standards of these studies have yet to be systematically evaluated. This study employs the CLARITY AI framework, a modular tool designed to assess both structural rigor and macro-topical completeness, to conduct the first longitudinal assessment of AI research in MG. An examination of 20 peer-reviewed studies published between 2020 and 2024 reveals an upward trend in average total scores, with significant improvements in evaluation metrics (+2.33), data availability (+1.32), and study type and objectives (+1.55) from 2021 to 2024. A subsequent robustness check affirms the consistency of these trends. Despite these advancements, critical gaps persist, particularly in usability testing, user engagement, and ethical reporting, where scores typically fell below 1. These findings suggest that while technological advancements are progressing, the translational readiness of AI applications for rare diseases is still hindered by the underreporting of human-centered and ethical aspects. This research offers practical recommendations and sets a benchmark for enhancing transparency, methodological rigor, and clinical relevance in AI applications for rare diseases. © The Author(s), under exclusive licence to Springer Science+Business Media, LLC, part of Springer Nature 2026.

In the era of pervasive machine learning (ML), its unbridled application may not consistently deliver anticipated advantages, potentially squandering resources, introducing unanticipated complexities, and even leading to failure. This study systematically investigates fundamental questions: Under what circumstances should ML be integrated, and which problem-solving workflows derive the greatest benefits? To address these questions, this paper critically evaluates existing decision-making frameworks and guidelines, identifying their strengths and weaknesses in determining ML applicability. A comprehensive literature review and analysis are conducted to delineate explicit criteria for assessing when ML is preferable over traditional engineering methods. Subsequently, a conceptual, structured decision-making framework is proposed, incorporating practical steps and checklists to aid engineers in method selection, model development, validation, and deployment. The paper also underscores critical challenges and potential future research questions in the field. © The Author(s) 2026.

In the realm of reaction modeling, the characterization of transition states (TS) is pivotal, yet traditional DFT approaches are computationally expensive. To address this issue, machine-learning interatomic potentials (MLIPs) offer quantum-level accuracy at a lower cost, but their effectiveness in TS optimization is limited due to a scarcity of large-scale Hessian data. This study introduces HORM, an extensive quantum-chemistry Hessian dataset for reactive systems, comprising 1.84 million matrices at the ωB97x/6-31G(d) level. To efficiently utilize second-order information, we propose Hessian-informed training with stochastic row sampling, a method that manages the computational overhead associated with incorporating Hessians. Across various MLIP architectures and force-learning schemes, HORM results in a reduction of up to 63% in Hessian mean absolute error and up to 200-fold improvement in TS-search efficiency compared to models trained without Hessians. By filling critical data and methodological gaps, HORM enables the development of more accurate, robust reactive MLIPs and facilitates scalable exploration of reaction networks. This research offers a significant advancement in the field, promising more efficient and accurate reaction modeling. © The Author(s) 2025.

This study introduces a novel dataset pertaining to the dynamics of underwater umbilicals associated with a Remotely Operated Vehicle (ROV), specifically the BlueROV2. The data was sourced from three distinct platforms: a Motion Capture System (mo-cap system), the onboard sensors of the BlueROV2, and a tension sensor. The mo-cap system monitors the motion of the ROV and its tether, while the tension sensor quantifies the force exerted by the cable tension on the surface side, near the tether drum. The dataset was amassed for research purposes, with a focus on investigating underwater cables in the context of modeling, estimation, and control using machine learning and data-driven control techniques. This dataset can function as a benchmark for the development and validation of underwater tether models, empowering researchers to create control methods that account for tether drag or entanglement, thereby enhancing the navigational precision of ROVs, or developing automated tether management systems. This paper outlines the experimental setup, data collection, and post-processing procedures. Additionally, it offers representative plots to emphasize the salient characteristics of the dataset. © The Author(s) 2025.

In the aviation industry, the gradual degradation of aircraft turbofan engines can pose significant risks to flight safety and escalate maintenance costs. This study introduces a Heterogeneous Ensemble Deep Neural Network (HEDNN) for the multi-class prediction of turbofan engine health status, aiming to enhance prognostic capabilities for predictive maintenance. The data collection spans over 12 years (2012-2024) from a dual-spool, variable geometry turbofan engine, with each run sampled at a consistent throttle setting. Raw sensor outputs were preprocessed using a robust z-score filter to eliminate outliers, a 35-sample moving median imputation to address missing temperature data, and the removal of non-numeric artifacts. The preprocessed dataset, comprising 97 predictive features post-FADEC upgrade standardization, was partitioned into training (80%) and testing (20%) sets, and categorized into three health classes based on performance margin (PMAR) and specific fuel consumption (SMAR) indices.

Three base learners were engineered: a bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network with 128 hidden units, a one-dimensional Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) with 11 convolution normalization Exponential Linear Unit (ELU) blocks, and a Bi-directional Gated Recurrent Unit (BiGRU) with four GRU layers and custom flip/concatenate stages. Each model was trained for up to 60 epochs, with a batch size of 16, using the Adam optimizer and piecewise learning rate decay (initial η = 0.01). The individual networks generated class probability vectors, which were combined in a shallow fully connected ensemble, and finalized by softmax classification. The performance of the HEDNN was evaluated using Root Mean Square Error (RMSE), Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE), overall accuracy, precision, recall, F1 score, and multi-class Area Under the

In the context of escalating student dropout rates in academic institutions, this study investigates the application of Artificial Intelligence (AI) to address this challenge, particularly focusing on the utilization of unlabeled data. Previous research has primarily employed AI models using administrative and academic data, neglecting personal student information. To mitigate this limitation, this research explores the potential of machine learning algorithms to extract valuable insights from such data. However, a critical hurdle in this approach is the scarcity of labeled data, which can be difficult to obtain due to its dispersal across multiple systems or the time-consuming process of collection, especially during the implementation of new academic programs. This data scarcity impedes institutions from fully leveraging AI's potential.

This paper presents a comparative analysis of various clustering algorithms to identify the most effective technique for uncovering factors contributing to university student attrition using unlabeled data. The novelty of this research lies not only in the algorithmic comparison but also in the integration of diverse data preprocessing methodologies. This integration streamlines the selection of the optimal combination, including advanced data transformations for the harmonization of numerical and categorical information.

The proposed methodology is validated through a real-world case study using academic data from a Spanish university. Additionally, an exploratory analysis is conducted to identify factors influencing cluster formation. The findings of this study can be applied to similar experiments where social or economic data are sparse, and the majority of available attributes are academic in nature. The empirical validation and insights gained from this study can potentially revolutionize the approach to student dropout prediction and retention strategies in academic institutions.

In the global context of Parkinson's disease (PD), a neurodegenerative disorder frequently associated with dysarthria, a speech disorder caused by neurological damage, this study presents an innovative, automated, and objective framework for classifying the severity of speech disorders in PD patients. The proposed methodology leverages acoustic features and machine learning techniques to address the limitations of subjective auditory evaluations, which are time-consuming and require specialized expertise.

The study analyzed speech recordings from 221 PD patients at the University Hospital of Kiel, Germany. The classification process involves two phases: the initial phase extracts frequency-domain features using linear predictive coding (LPC), mel-frequency cepstral coefficients (MFCCs), and discrete wavelet packet decomposition (DWPD). Subsequently, feature matching is performed using k-nearest neighbors (KNN), support vector machines (SVM), and neural networks (NN). A novel segment-to-subject voting strategy is introduced, where voice records are segmented into short, 2-second segments, individually analyzed, and classified, with final subject-level predictions derived by majority voting across segments.

The ensemble-style approach significantly improves classification accuracy, with DWPD combined with KNN achieving an accuracy of 89.7% and a macro-F1 score of 89.3% in a three-class (mild, moderate, severe) system. The successful implementation of this methodology in clinical settings could potentially improve the treatment of speech disorders in PD patients by offering an objective, efficient, reliable, and user-friendly assessment tool. This tool can aid clinicians in more effective patient monitoring, facilitating faster, more consistent evaluations, particularly during therapy follow-ups and in telemedicine applications.

In the realm of emotion recognition, Micro-Expression Recognition (MER) holds significant importance due to its potential in deciphering human emotions. However, the transient and subtle nature of micro-expressions often poses challenges to their accurate identification. This study proposes a cutting-edge MER framework, combining Calibrated Regression with Maximum Mean Discrepancy (MMD), Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning (MAML), and Meta-Reinforcement Learning (Meta-RL) to enhance recognition precision and adaptability. A 2D Convolutional Neural Network (2DCNN) serves as the foundation for feature extraction, effectively capturing minute spatial details of Facial Expressions (FEs). To tackle the issue of feature misalignment, a Heteroscedastic Neural Network (HNN) is developed for predictive uncertainty estimation. The framework employs a two-stage learning process, with Negative Log-Likelihood (NLL) optimization refining model parameters and MMD ensuring improved alignment between micro- and macro-expressions. Furthermore, the Meta-RL framework optimizes feature learning by characterizing the optimal gap of stationary points achieved using MAML and enhancing generalization. Experiments on standard datasets such as SAMM and CASME II demonstrate the superiority of the proposed approach, yielding 96.74% accuracy on SAMM and 98.84% accuracy on CASME II, outperforming existing models. The results underscore the model's robustness, adaptability, and efficacy, positioning it as an ideal choice for real-world micro-expression analysis applications. © The Author(s) 2025.

This study offers an in-depth review of the progress in Arabic Word Sense Disambiguation (WSD), with a primary focus on contextualized methods, notably deep learning and transformer models, in the face of the linguistic intricacies and data scarcity inherent in Arabic language processing. The critical role of WSD in Natural Language Processing (NLP) is underscored, as it continues to pose a significant challenge. This paper systematically explores traditional knowledge-based methods, machine learning approaches, deep learning models, and hybrid techniques, with a special emphasis on transformer models, such as BERT, which have recently demonstrated exceptional performance in Arabic WSD. The selection criteria for models are delineated, encompassing their performance on benchmark datasets and their applicability to real-world scenarios. The paper provides a comparative evaluation of the methodologies, elucidating their strengths and weaknesses through the lens of Accuracy, Precision, Recall, and F1-score. Unlike previous surveys, this work offers a holistic analysis that integrates multiple evaluation metrics across various studies. The paper concludes by identifying key obstacles, including the need for expanded annotated datasets and enhanced contextual comprehension, while proposing potential avenues for future research, including domain adaptation and cross-dialectal WSD. © The Author(s), under exclusive licence to Springer Nature Switzerland AG 2025.

In the realm of machine learning applications, large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated versatility across numerous sectors. However, their implementation in healthcare, specifically in the pharmaceutical domain, presents unique challenges, particularly in relation to the precise and trustworthy communication of contraindications. This study proposes an innovative solution to enhance the efficiency of LLMs in handling contraindications by incorporating a Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline. Leveraging OpenAI's GPT-4o-mini as the foundation model and the text-embedding-3-small model for embeddings, our approach employs LangChain to manage a hybrid retrieval system with re-ranking. This system capitalizes on Drug Utilization Review (DUR) data from public databases, focusing on contraindications specific to age groups, pregnancy, and concurrent drug usage. The dataset encompasses 300 question-answer pairs categorized into three groups, with the baseline model accuracy ranging from 0.49 to 0.57. Upon the integration of the RAG pipeline, we observed a substantial improvement in model accuracy, attaining rates of 0.94, 0.87, and 0.89 for contraindications related to age groups, pregnancy, and concurrent drug use, respectively. These findings suggest that the augmentation of LLMs with a RAG framework can significantly diminish uncertainties in prescription and drug intake decisions by delivering more accurate and dependable drug contraindication information. © The Author(s), under exclusive licence to Springer Nature Switzerland AG 2026.

In the realm of machine learning, reducing the dimensionality of a dataset while maintaining its intrinsic dynamics, particularly for nonlinear and time-dependent processes, poses a significant challenge. To address this issue, we introduce a novel dimension reduction technique, termed Average Volatility Dimensioning (AVD). This method captures the interplay between features and condenses multiple dimensions into a univariate signal. This article delves into the development of the AVD mechanism and compares its performance against nine contemporary dimension reduction techniques. The efficacy of the reduced dimensions is assessed by employing them in machine learning classification tasks and comparing the resulting phase-space portraits. The validation is carried out on eight diverse datasets, encompassing nonlinear time series data from domains such as movement recognition, fault detection, and environmental monitoring. The findings indicate that AVD most effectively encapsulates the data dynamics, demonstrating superior performance in the discussed machine learning approach, while maintaining an interpretable phase-space trajectory, resisting noise, and remaining computationally efficient. © The Author(s) 2025.

In the realm of code auditing, while an array of sophisticated and established models and methods exist, the interpretability of these models continues to pose a substantial challenge. Machine learning models, designed to tackle code auditing issues, are capable of identifying vulnerable code, yet they lack transparency in their decision-making processes or the ability to confirm whether they have accurately captured the traits of vulnerable code. To appraise the capacity of such models in extracting vulnerability-related features, this study introduces Program-PLATE, a method that expands a single vulnerable file into a PLATE-dataset, thereby facilitating a more objective evaluation of the model's performance on the PLATE-dataset. This method was employed to assess multiple models, and the results were subjected to a comprehensive analysis. The findings are discussed, and potential research directions for future investigations are proposed. © The Author(s) 2026.

In the contemporary landscape of customer analytics, the integration of Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) has garnered significant attention due to the demand for trust, transparency, and actionable insights in predictive modeling. This systematic literature review investigates the application of XAI techniques, such as SHAP and LIME, alongside other AI models like machine learning, deep learning, and ensemble models, in areas such as churn prediction, customer segmentation, and retention strategies. The research was conducted through a structured search across databases like Scopus, IEEE, and other digital libraries, resulting in the analysis of 80 studies published between 2015 and 2025.

The review categorizes XAI approaches based on their industry application, interpretability findings, and methodology, highlighting their role in enhancing model decision-making and transparency. The findings suggest that XAI not only boosts stakeholder trust but also facilitates more ethical and targeted customer interventions. A comparative study was also conducted on XAI techniques for churn prediction, customer segmentation, and retention. The study reveals limitations in real-time deployment, standardization, and evaluation metrics, which are discussed in the gaps and discussions section, along with potential future research directions for XAI models, hybrid models, and domain-specific explainability contexts. © The Author(s), under exclusive licence to Springer Nature Switzerland AG 2026.

In the age of exponentially growing data, the need for efficient statistical methods to identify crucial variables is paramount, particularly in establishing relationships between predictors and response variables. This study focuses on the challenge of achieving this goal while managing the False Discovery Rate (FDR) and preserving statistical power. To address this, we adopt the knockoff filter, a novel approach that generates inexpensive knockoff variables, mimicking the correlation structure of the original variables, acting as negative controls for inference. We extend the application of knockoffs to Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM), a swift and precise machine learning algorithm. Shapley Additive Explanations (SHAP) values are utilized to decipher the opaque nature of machine learning. Our method, through extensive experimentation, demonstrates superior performance compared to traditional approaches, effectively identifying vital variables for each class. It also offers enhanced speed and efficiency across various datasets. To substantiate our approach, an extensive simulation study is undertaken. The integration of knockoffs into LightGBM significantly improves performance and interpretability, contributing to the evolution of variable selection methods. This research aims to tackle the challenges of variable selection in the big data era, offering a valuable instrument for identifying pertinent variables in statistical modeling and machine learning applications. © The Author(s) 2025.

This study introduces a machine learning framework utilizing a large language model (LLM) to expedite the discovery of organic solar cell (OSC) materials. The primary challenge in materials discovery lies in the scarcity of informatics-ready data and the intricacy of numerical descriptors, compounded by the dispersed nature of scientific knowledge across publications. The proposed framework addresses this issue by extracting structured data from literature and predicting device performance using natural language embeddings.

The LLM was fine-tuned on a curated dataset of 422 OSC devices, demonstrating robust predictive accuracy across critical performance metrics: power conversion efficiency (PCE, R2: 0.87), short-circuit current (J<inf>SC</inf>, R2: 0.82), open-circuit voltage (V<inf>OC</inf>, R2: 0.89), and fill factor (FF, R2: 0.59). Subsequently, the models were employed to investigate a vast space of 1.4 million material combinations, experimental variables, and device architectures.

The analysis of this extensive data set led to the generation of data-driven design guidelines, highlighting optimal donor-acceptor combinations and processing conditions that consistently enhance device performance. The findings are visualized in a presented figure. The proposed LLM-driven framework offers a promising approach to accelerate OSC materials discovery and optimize device performance.

In this study, we develop an automated machine learning (AutoML) framework to predict case durations and delays in lower courts across India, providing essential assistance to judicial stakeholders and administrators. Utilizing a comprehensive dataset of over 22.4 million criminal cases, we employ a tree-based ensemble model, augmented with explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) techniques, to forecast case delays and durations with a focus on interpretability. By analyzing key variables such as case type, existing backlog, and jurisdiction type, available at the time of case filing, we systematically examine their impact on case length and delays.

To improve prediction accuracy, we replace a single regression model with two dedicated models, one for on-time cases and another for delayed cases, resulting in reduced prediction errors. The RMSE for on-time cases decreased from 8.33 to 4.90 months, while for delayed cases, it dropped from 23.32 to 13.79 months. Additionally, a separate classification model enhances the delay prediction model, achieving 80% accuracy with a weighted F1 score of 84.1%.

Moreover, a hybrid classification-regression framework shows promise for improved delay and duration prediction in end-to-end simulations. The models exhibit robust predictive capability, indicating the potential of AI algorithms in enhancing efficiency and timeliness in judicial processes. Furthermore, the application of XAI provides insightful explanations for predictions, ensuring that stakeholders understand the reasoning behind predicted durations.

These predictive models can support case management, promote equitable judicial processes by identifying areas prone to delays, and inform policies aimed at enhancing the efficiency, timeliness, and transparency of the Indian judiciary. The dataset and AutoML model code are made available for further research and reproducibility of results. © The Author(s) 2025.

In the medical field, patients suffering from unstable pelvic fractures concurrent with traumatic brain injury (TBI) pose complex clinical challenges due to the conflicting treatment requirements of controlled fluid resuscitation and maintaining cerebral perfusion pressure. This study retrospectively analyzed 204 cases of patients with such injuries between January 1, 2010, and May 31, 2025. A novel Nomogram model was developed using Least Absolute Shrinkage and Selection Operator (LASSO) and multivariate logistic regression, alongside the creation of an interpretable machine learning prediction model. The findings revealed shock, abnormal systolic blood pressure (SBP), non-surgical management, and prolonged coagulation time as independent predictors of poor prognosis. The Nomogram, constructed using SBP, shock, pelvic fracture surgery, and prothrombin time (PT), demonstrated commendable performance (AUC = 0.801, C-index = 0.7747). The risk of poor prognosis was minimized at SBP levels between 110-122 mmHg, with a U-shaped correlation observed between SBP and prognosis. The developed Nomogram and interpretable XGBoost model offer a foundation for personalized blood pressure management, coagulation monitoring, and surgical decision-making. The study, registered under ChiCTR2500103926 on May 9, 2025, with PID: 275507, underscores the potential of machine learning in individualized treatment strategies for patients with unstable pelvic fractures and TBI.

This research introduces an explicable machine learning method for categorizing traditional Korean ceramics, such as celadon, buncheong, and white porcelain, utilizing X-ray fluorescence chemical composition data. A meticulously compiled dataset consisting of 624 samples underwent analysis using six machine learning algorithms: Principal Component Analysis-Linear Discriminant Analysis, Decision Tree, Random Forest, Extreme Gradient Boosting, k-Nearest Neighbors, and Support Vector Machine. The tree-based Random Forest and Extreme Gradient Boosting models exhibited the highest classification accuracy of 95.8%. All models successfully identified white porcelain, while celadon and buncheong experienced partial misclassification due to shared chemical attributes. The interpretability of the models was augmented through Shapley Additive Explanations, revealing Fe<inf>2</inf>O<inf>3</inf> and TiO<inf>2</inf> as the primary factors influencing type differentiation, in accordance with established ceramic coloration mechanisms. These findings underscore the utility of explicable machine learning for chemical-based ceramic classification and offer a quantitative framework that complements traditional typological methods in cultural heritage research. © The Author(s) 2026.

In the realm of polymer science, adjusting solution viscosity is a crucial functional application, yet the intricate interplay between molecular chemistry, polymer architecture, and intermolecular interactions poses challenges in tailoring precise rheological responses. This study proposes a computational framework that combines topology-aware generative machine learning, Gaussian process modeling, and multiparticle collision dynamics to engineer polymers with customizable shear-rate-dependent viscosity profiles. Thirty rheological profiles of varying complexity were targeted, and Bayesian optimization was employed to identify polymers that met all low- and most medium-difficulty targets by modifying topology and solvophobicity, while maintaining other variables constant. In the low- and medium-difficulty regimes, design degeneracy was observed, where distinct polymers produced nearly identical rheological profiles. However, satisfying high-difficulty targets necessitated extrapolation beyond the initial design space, which was guided by physical scaling theories. This integrated framework offers a data-driven yet mechanistic approach to rational polymer design, establishing a promising route for the development of tailor-made polymers with desired rheological properties. © The Author(s) 2025.

In the era of Business Intelligence (BI), safeguarding sensitive data, particularly in the context of cloud-based outsourced computing, assumes paramount importance. This study proposes an AI-driven, automated solution aimed at concealing sensitive data while preserving its analytical integrity. The proposed solution employs an anonymization pipeline that integrates technologies such as pseudonymization, data masking, and machine learning for sensitive data identification. Our experimental results indicate that anonymized data maintains its analytical efficacy with negligible performance and accuracy degradation, thereby offering a robust basis for secure and efficient BI operations in cloud-based computing environments. This research contributes significantly to the development of secure BI practices in the era of outsourced computing. © The Author(s) 2026.

In the context of Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Steatohepatitis (MASH), a condition associated with a tenfold increase in liver-related mortality, the scarcity of reliable predictive biomarkers poses a significant challenge. This study introduces the MASHRisk score, a non-invasive diagnostic tool derived from a combination of routine clinical and biochemical panels, as a potential solution. Utilizing ten distinct machine learning algorithms, the MASHRisk score was developed based on data from 218 participants and subsequently validated across four cohorts (93, 96, and 26,256 individuals). The MASHRisk score demonstrated robust diagnostic performance, with Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic Curve (AUC) values ranging from 0.791 to 0.806 across the training, validation, and test sets. The MASHRisk score emerged as an independent predictor of MASH (p < 0.001) and outperformed existing indices such as Fibrosis-4 (FIB-4), Aspartate Aminotransferase to Platelet Ratio Index (APRI), Aspartate Aminotransferase to Alanine Aminotransferase Ratio (AAR), and Non-Alcoholic Fatty Liver Disease Fibrosis Score (NFS). In a prognostic cohort of 390,574 individuals, high-risk participants exhibited significantly elevated hazard ratios for liver-related mortality (HR: 12.296), MASH (HR: 12.829), cirrhosis (HR: 8.863), hepatocellular carcinoma (HR: 9.278), Atherosclerotic Cardiovascular Disease (ASCVD)-related mortality (HR: 2.303), and all-cause mortality (HR: 1.744

In the realm of computer science, there is an urgent need for a non-invasive, computer-based method to detect chronic liver diseases, which are frequently asymptomatic, progressive, and potentially fatal. Machine Learning (ML) techniques can significantly contribute to the understanding and prediction of liver diseases, a widespread health concern. This study emphasizes the importance of data preprocessing in ML classification, as it ensures data cleanliness and standardization, thereby improving model performance by addressing issues such as missing values, feature scaling, and categorical encoding. This process minimizes the risk of biased or inaccurate predictions.

This research represents one of the first comprehensive evaluations of the impact of normalization, imputation methods, and feature encoding on multiple ML models for liver disease prediction. We systematically compare various preprocessing pipelines (scaling, imputation, feature encoding) across five common machine learning classifiers. To quantify model performance variance due to preprocessing changes, we introduce a preprocessing sensitivity index (PSI).

Our findings reveal that specific preprocessing strategies consistently outperform others in liver disease datasets, insights not previously reported. Post-preprocessing, a significant increase in prediction accuracy is observed, with an improvement of approximately 8-12%. This study underscores the role of preprocessing in mitigating data inconsistencies and noise, and enhancing the model's ability to detect subtle patterns within the data.

In the realm of acute ischemic stroke management, the precise localization of perfusion deficits in diffusion-weighted MRI (DWI) plays a pivotal role. However, existing deep learning techniques often produce discrete binary masks, thereby disregarding the continuous nature of ischemic injury and losing valuable intra-lesion information. This study introduces StrokeFlow, a novel framework that addresses this limitation by representing the ischemic region as a continuous field of ischemic density. The proposed coordinate-based network is designed to output a smooth ischemic density field, providing voxel-level infarction probability estimates. Additionally, a vector flow head is incorporated, which is supervised to learn a vector field that aligns with the negative gradient of the Apparent Diffusion Coefficient (ADC) map, thus modeling the directionality of the perfusion deficit. The performance of StrokeFlow was evaluated on the ISLES 2022 public dataset, where it demonstrated superior lesion boundary accuracy and significantly outperformed strong baselines in the 95% Hausdorff Distance metric. Moreover, StrokeFlow exhibited enhanced sensitivity in detecting small and multifocal lesions. By transitioning from discrete segmentation to continuous, functionally-aware fields, StrokeFlow offers a more biologically plausible and interpretable tool for a comprehensive clinical assessment of ischemic stroke, potentially revolutionizing stroke management strategies. © The Author(s) 2025.

In the contemporary era, the proliferation of smart devices and connected vehicles has led to an exponential increase in mobile data traffic, exerting significant strain on wireless networks, particularly in terms of ensuring quality of service and managing handover (HO) processes, especially in high-speed vehicular environments. To address these challenges, vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) communication has emerged as a crucial element of next-generation intelligent transportation systems. However, maintaining seamless mobility in such environments remains a formidable task, given the high frequency of HOs. This paper offers a comprehensive review of HO decision techniques specifically designed for V2V communications in 6G networks. The study delves into the core challenges, including mobility management, security, interference, and service continuity, and assesses the performance of state-of-the-art HO algorithms, such as Bayesian regression, fuzzy logic, machine learning, and software-defined networking approaches. Furthermore, the paper underscores the potential of 6G technologies, including ultra-reliable low-latency communication, terahertz spectrum, and AI-native networking, to enhance the stability, efficiency, and reliability of V2V HO management. Lastly, the paper provides insights into ongoing research challenges and future directions, emphasizing the role of advanced HO mechanisms in supporting the demands of autonomous driving and intelligent transportation systems. © The Author(s) 2025.

In the realm of pharmaceuticals, food consumption can significantly influence drug absorption, thereby impacting both the efficacy and safety of administered drugs. Predicting these food-drug interactions (FDI) is a complex task, as traditional methods, such as in vitro and in vivo models, often fall short due to the biological variability inherent in the gastrointestinal system. This study explores the predictive capacity and precision of machine learning (ML) in FDI prediction, comparing it to conventional methods. The focus is on understanding how ML models leverage food dataset information to optimize drug formulation and dosing.

The review delves into recent advancements in FDI prediction, elucidating the underlying mechanisms and their effects on drug bioavailability. Various ML techniques, including supervised and unsupervised learning, as well as reinforcement learning, are analyzed in the context of absorption, distribution, metabolism, and elimination (ADME) forecasting and drug development.

While ML shows promise in overcoming the limitations of traditional methods, challenges persist, such as data quality, model generalizability, and integration into the drug development process. This review discusses potential solutions, including the integration of ML with other emerging technologies, such as PBPK modeling, to enhance its potential in drug development.

Furthermore, the study examines the prospects of deep learning, explainable AI, and ethical and legal aspects of applying ML in pharmacokinetics. It also highlights the interdisciplinary approaches necessary to improve patient care outcomes. © The Author(s) 2025.
